post.topic: essential Claude API optimization techniques for developers
post.hook: 4 Claude API tricks that will save you hours of debugging time
post.caption:
4 Claude API tricks that will save you hours of debugging time

Save this carousel for your next Claude API project.

slides.title: Use Cache Control
slides.body: Cache repeated prompts with cache_control to reduce latency and costs. Perfect for system prompts and contexts you reuse.

slides.title: Stream Long Responses
slides.body: Use streaming for better UX with long responses. Users see content immediately instead of waiting for completion.
slides.code_snippet:
stream = client.messages.create(
  model="claude-3-sonnet-20240229",
  messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
  stream=True
)

slides.title: Handle Rate Limits
slides.body: Implement exponential backoff for rate limit errors. Prevents request failures and maintains smooth operation.

slides.title: Set Max Tokens
slides.body: Always specify max_tokens to control response length and costs. Prevents unexpectedly long outputs from breaking your budget.
slides.code_snippet:
response = client.messages.create(
  model="claude-3-sonnet-20240229",
  max_tokens=1000,
  messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
)

slides.title: Save This Post!
slides.body: Follow @ask.claudeai for more Claude API tips and save this for your next project.
